people.text: Lily wants a quiet restaurant for a birthday dinner with her parents. Her mother is vegetarian, and they would prefer somewhere where they can book a table in advance. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Adam wants cheap food after football practice. He needs somewhere open late and wants large portions rather than a formal meal. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Priya wants to try seafood near the harbour. She would like outdoor tables and needs the menu to include some non-fish options for her friend. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

George wants a restaurant for a work lunch. It must serve quickly, offer online ordering before arrival and be close to the business district. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

Marta wants a relaxed cafÃ©-style place for Sunday brunch. She needs gluten-free choices and would like somewhere that welcomes dogs. They also want clear information before deciding, so they can choose with confidence.

texts.text: Rose Garden Dining is a calm restaurant with bookable tables and soft background music. The menu includes several vegetarian main courses, as well as meat and fish dishes. Staff are used to family celebrations and can arrange small birthday desserts. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Goal Burger stays open until midnight and serves inexpensive burgers, wraps and chips in generous portions. It is popular with sports teams after evening practice. Customers order at the counter, and the atmosphere is noisy but friendly. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Harbour Plate has outdoor tables overlooking the boats and specialises in fresh seafood. There are also chicken, pasta and salad dishes for guests who do not eat fish. Booking is recommended on sunny weekends. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Quick Desk Kitchen is in the business district and focuses on fast lunch service. Office workers can order online before they arrive, then collect meals or sit down briefly. Most dishes are ready within ten minutes. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Sunday Spoon serves brunch until 2 p.m. and has a relaxed cafÃ© atmosphere. The menu marks gluten-free pancakes, eggs and cakes clearly. Dogs are welcome at the covered outside tables, where water bowls are provided. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Sea Salt Grill is famous for seafood, but it has no outdoor seating and only one vegetarian salad. It is beside the harbour and popular with visitors, though queues are long during the summer. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

Budget Bowl offers cheap noodles and rice dishes, but it closes at 8 p.m. and portions are small. It is a good choice for a quick snack, not for hungry people after evening sport. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

City Formal is an elegant restaurant near the offices. Lunch is excellent but slow, and customers cannot order ahead. It suits long business meals rather than people who need to return to work quickly. Visitors receive clear information before booking, and staff are happy to answer simple questions. The service is easy to understand, with practical details clearly explained for new users.

answers.explanation: Lily needs a quiet bookable restaurant with vegetarian options; A fits.
Adam needs cheap late food and large portions after sport; B matches.
Priya wants harbour seafood, outdoor seating and non-fish options; C fits.
George needs fast work lunch, online ordering and business district location; D matches.
Marta needs Sunday brunch, gluten-free choices and dog-friendly seating; E fits.
